title: Detecting 16S rRNA Gene Fragments from a Metagenome to Assemble Full-Length 16S Sequences
author: Benjamin Tully
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.d7u9nv

steps.title: Convert FASTQ to FASTA
steps.instruction: Convert quality trimmed FASTQ metagenomic sequences to a FASTA format. Assumes that sequences are paired-end. fq2fa is available as part of IDBA-UD. Example command: fq2fa --merge --filter INFILE_NAME.R1.001_paired.fastq INFILE_NAME.R2.001_paired.fastq INFILE_NAME.001.merged.fasta. Parameters: --merge = interweave paired-end sequences in two separate FASTQ files; --filter = remove sequences containing Ns.

steps.title: Detect SSU rRNA Gene Fragments
steps.instruction: Detect small subunit (SSU) rRNA gene fragments using command-line based Meta-RNA. This example utilizes a specific version (accessible via http://weizhong-lab.ucsd.edu/meta_rna/rRNA_hmm3.tar.gz). Example command: rna_hmm3.py -i INFILE_NAME.001.merged.fasta -L /directory/location/of/HMM/files/rna_hmm3/HMM3/ -o OUTFILE_NAME.001_predictedRNAs -m ssu -e .0000000001 -p 36. Parameters: -L = provide the directory address of the downloaded, pre-computed HMM alignment models for both SSU and large subunit rRNA genes; -o = set name of output file; -m = sets target as SSU fragments only; -e = E-value cutoff used by HMMER (1 x 10^-10); -p = number of available CPUs.

steps.title: Trim Original FASTQ Files
steps.instruction: Use the script MetaRNA_to_FastQ.py to trim the original quality trimmed FASTQ files based on the output table created by Meta-RNA. MetaRNA_to_FastQ.py is only confirmed to work with the version of Meta-RNA described above and requires Biopython. Example command: MetaRNA_to_FastQ.py -r OUTFILE_NAME.001_predictedRNAs -q INFILE_NAME.R1.001_paired.fastq -o OUTFILE_PREFIX1.001. Parameters: -r = Meta-RNA table output; -q = original FASTQ file searched by Meta-RNA; -o = a prefix for the outfile, the final file will have '.metagenome16S.fastq' as the suffix.

steps.title: Trim R2 Reads
steps.instruction: Repeat Step 2 (trimming original quality trimmed FASTQ files) for R2 reads (and any other sets of reads from the same sample) using MetaRNA_to_FastQ.py. Example command: MetaRNA_to_FastQ.py -r OUTFILE_NAME.001_predictedRNAs -q INFILE_NAME.R2.001_paired.fastq -o OUTFILE_PREFIX2.001.

steps.title: Combine Output FASTQ Files
steps.instruction: Combine output 'metagenome16S.fastq' files as desired. Options: (1) Pairs of 16S rRNA fragments, or (2) All 16S rRNA fragments from a single sample.

steps.title: Set up EMIRGE Dependencies
steps.instruction: If using EMIRGE for the first time, set up its dependencies: Cython, pysam, scipy/numpy, usearch, samtools, bowtie.

steps.title: Download EMIRGE Reference Database
steps.instruction: If using EMIRGE for the first time, a reference database is required. SILVA (Ref111) can be downloaded using the script provided with EMIRGE: emirge_download_candidate_db.py.

steps.title: Correct EMIRGE Reference Database
steps.instruction: If using EMIRGE for the first time, the reference database must be 'corrected' using the script provided with EMIRGE: fix_nonstandard_chars.py.

steps.title: Index Reference Database with Bowtie
steps.instruction: If using EMIRGE for the first time, the reference database must be indexed using bowtie. Example command: bowtie-build SSU_candidate_db.fasta SSU_candidate_db_btindex.

steps.title: Assemble Full-Length 16S rRNA Sequences with EMIRGE
steps.instruction: Assemble full-length 16S rRNA sequences using EMIRGE via emirge_amplicon.py, as the input file contains exclusively 16S rRNA fragments. Example command: emirge_amplicon.py ./emirgeWorkingDir -1 OUTFILE_NAME_all.metagenome16S.fastq -f /directory/location/of/EMIRGE/SSURef_111_candidate_db.fasta -b /directoy/location/of/EMIRGE/SSURef_111_candidate_db_btindex -l 260 -i 246 -s 88 -a 32 --phred33. Parameters: ./emirgeWorkingDir = creates a working directory for EMIRGE command (delete or rename if re-running); -1 = input FASTQ file containing all metagenome16S.fastq sequences; -f = location of reference FASTA file; -b = location of reference FASTA bowtie index file; -l = max length of sequences; -i = insert size between sequences; -s = standard deviation of insert sequence sizes; -a = number of available CPUs; --phred33 = utilizes 33 based Phred quality.

steps.title: Create Final FASTA File
steps.instruction: Move to the 'emirgeWorkingDir' and create the final FASTA file using the script provided with EMIRGE: emirge_rename_fasta.py.

steps.title: Optional: Assign Taxonomy with mothur
steps.instruction: This is an optional step to assign taxonomy using mothur (version 1.36.1). It requires a reference alignment for align.seqs and reference FASTA + taxonomy files for classify.seqs. Run as three separate commands: 1. align.seqs(fasta=INPUT.16S.fasta, reference=reference.alignment.fasta, flip=T, processors=64); 2. remove.seqs(accnos=INPUT.16S.flip.accnos, fasta=INPUT.16S.align); 3. classify.seqs(fasta=INPUT.16S.pick.align, template=reference.database.fasta, taxonomy=reference.database.tax, cutoff=80, iters=1000, processors=64). Parameters: flip = T - looks for matches in both forward and reverse directions; processors = available number of CPUs; cutoff = level cutoff for a taxonomic assignment; iters = number of comparisons performed to ensure correct assignment.
